name: Overdrive router benchmark
description: A lightweight benchmark pack for checking whether skill-router selects useful, narrow skill sets across common Overdrive tasks.

scoring.manualProtocol: Run each controlPrompt and routedPrompt in fresh sessions with the same model, same repository state, and same available tools. Score the outputs blind where practical. This file validates routing coverage; it does not claim automatic output-quality proof by itself.

scoreMeanings.1: Partially useful but incomplete, generic, or weakly verified.
scoreMeanings.2: Strong, specific, scoped, and verified for the task.

cases.prompt: Build a premium Next.js landing page for a developer-tool startup with polished interactions and verify it in a browser.
cases.controlPrompt: Build a premium Next.js landing page for a developer-tool startup with polished interactions and verify it in a browser.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then build a premium Next.js landing page for a developer-tool startup with polished interactions and verify it in a browser.
rubric: Chooses a concrete visual direction instead of generic SaaS defaults.
Builds the actual usable page rather than a marketing explanation.
Includes interaction polish such as hover, focus, responsive, and motion states.
Uses real browser validation or clearly explains why it could not run.
Keeps the implementation scoped to the requested page.

cases.prompt: Make this mobile drawer feel fluid, interruptible, and natural when dragged or flicked.
cases.controlPrompt: Make this mobile drawer feel fluid, interruptible, and natural when dragged or flicked.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then make this mobile drawer feel fluid, interruptible, and natural when dragged or flicked.
rubric: Identifies gesture velocity, snap points, interruptibility, and reduced-motion concerns.
Uses spring or physically plausible motion instead of only duration/easing tweaks.
Preserves accessibility and keyboard/touch behavior.
Avoids over-animating unrelated UI.

cases.prompt: This popover animation feels sluggish and cheap. Tune it so it feels crisp on web.
cases.controlPrompt: This popover animation feels sluggish and cheap. Tune it so it feels crisp on web.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then tune this sluggish popover animation so it feels crisp on web.
rubric: Separates web easing/duration polish from gesture-heavy fluid animation.
Considers transform origin, opacity, scale, timing, and focus behavior.
Keeps motion subtle enough for repeated product use.
Includes reduced-motion handling.

cases.prompt: Refactor this checkout flow across several files, but keep behavior identical and verify it.
cases.controlPrompt: Refactor this checkout flow across several files, but keep behavior identical and verify it.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then refactor this checkout flow across several files while preserving behavior and verification.
rubric: Inspects the existing code before editing.
States the refactor boundary and behavior-preservation criteria.
Uses a short implementation plan with verification checkpoints.
Avoids unrelated cleanup or broad rewrites.

cases.prompt: Add a smarter onboarding thing to my app.
cases.controlPrompt: Add a smarter onboarding thing to my app.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then handle this ambiguous request: add a smarter onboarding thing to my app.
rubric: Does not silently assume what smarter onboarding means.
Offers concrete options or asks focused questions when necessary.
Recognizes questionnaire onboarding as a likely domain.
Keeps the first implementation phase small and testable.

cases.prompt: Security review this PR for auth, XSS, injection, secrets, and supply-chain issues.
cases.controlPrompt: Security review this PR for auth, XSS, injection, secrets, and supply-chain issues.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then security review this PR for auth, XSS, injection, secrets, and supply-chain issues.
rubric: Prioritizes concrete vulnerabilities over style commentary.
Checks auth/authz, injection, XSS, secrets, and dependency risk.
Uses file and line references where possible.
Distinguishes confirmed findings from residual risks.

cases.prompt: What has the React community been saying about server components over the last 30 days?
cases.controlPrompt: What has the React community been saying about server components over the last 30 days?
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then research what the React community has been saying about server components over the last 30 days.
rubric: Treats the request as time-boxed recent community research.
Separates community sentiment from official documentation.
Cites sources and dates when browsing is available.
Avoids stale claims presented as current.

cases.prompt: Create a Jack Roberts style 3D scroll website for a moving company with an animated van hero and SEO-ready pages.
cases.controlPrompt: Create a Jack Roberts style 3D scroll website for a moving company with an animated van hero and SEO-ready pages.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then create a Jack Roberts style 3D scroll website for a moving company with an animated van hero and SEO-ready pages.
rubric: Breaks the work into brand research, asset prompts, scroll implementation, SEO, and launch checks.
Treats external tools such as image/video generators, Firecrawl, GitHub, and Vercel as optional.
Avoids copying a real client site too closely or making unsupported brand claims.
Includes performance, mobile, reduced-motion, and browser validation.

cases.prompt: Create start-frame, exploded end-frame, and video transition prompts for a product scroll-stop hero.
cases.controlPrompt: Create start-frame, exploded end-frame, and video transition prompts for a product scroll-stop hero.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then create start-frame, exploded end-frame, and video transition prompts for a product scroll-stop hero.
rubric: Creates coordinated prompts where start, end, and transition match.
Includes constraints for clean edges, consistent lighting, and brand fit.
Separates prompt writing from actual image/video generation.
Gives iteration guidance instead of promising a perfect first output.

cases.prompt: Run a pre-launch checklist for this SaaS before I share it publicly.
cases.controlPrompt: Run a pre-launch checklist for this SaaS before I share it publicly.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then run a pre-launch checklist for this SaaS before I share it publicly.
rubric: Covers readiness beyond code: monitoring, billing, support, privacy, rollback, and analytics.
Identifies security and privacy items without overloading unrelated skills.
Prioritizes blockers vs nice-to-haves.
Produces an actionable launch-day or first-week plan.

cases.prompt: Write launch emails for my product that do not sound like AI.
cases.controlPrompt: Write launch emails for my product that do not sound like AI.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then write launch emails for my product that do not sound like AI.
rubric: Uses product/audience context or asks for missing essentials.
Writes concrete, non-generic email copy.
Removes obvious AI phrasing and over-polished filler.
Includes useful variants or subject lines without bloating the answer.

cases.prompt: Turn this project research into a JSON Canvas map with grouped themes and useful connections.
cases.controlPrompt: Turn this project research into a JSON Canvas map with grouped themes and useful connections.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then turn this project research into a JSON Canvas map with grouped themes and useful connections.
rubric: Recognizes the task as JSON Canvas work rather than generic Markdown notes.
Creates a valid canvas structure with nodes, groups, and meaningful edges.
Preserves the original research meaning and source context.
Warns before broad edits to a real vault or project knowledge base.

cases.prompt: Build a high-quality MCP server for this API and use current SDK docs.
cases.controlPrompt: Build a high-quality MCP server for this API and use current SDK docs.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then build a high-quality MCP server for this API and use current SDK docs.
rubric: Recognizes MCP server design as the core task.
Uses current official SDK documentation where available.
Designs narrow, typed tools with clear schemas and error behavior.
Avoids exposing credentials or making live-service changes without approval.

cases.prompt: Create a Slack-ready animated emoji GIF for a team launch celebration.
cases.controlPrompt: Create a Slack-ready animated emoji GIF for a team launch celebration.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then create a Slack-ready animated emoji GIF for a team launch celebration.
rubric: Uses Slack-specific constraints for file size, dimensions, and loop length.
Designs a simple readable animation that works at small sizes.
Validates or describes validation steps for the final GIF.
Approval-gates any upload or posting action.

cases.prompt: Take over this existing app, understand the current product, redesign the onboarding and dashboard, preserve auth behavior, add smooth interactions, security review the risky paths, verify in browser, and leave a handoff.
cases.controlPrompt: Take over this existing app, understand the current product, redesign the onboarding and dashboard, preserve auth behavior, add smooth interactions, security review the risky paths, verify in browser, and leave a handoff.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, use ovd-workflow if available, then take over this existing app, understand the current product, redesign the onboarding and dashboard, preserve auth behavior, add smooth interactions, security review risky paths, verify in browser, and leave a handoff.
rubric: Does not force the task into a three-skill cap when the work is clearly multi-phase.
Phases skill usage so planning, design, security, and browser verification do not all flood context at once.
Uses ovd-workflow or an equivalent handoff mechanism when available without treating it as a routed skill.
Preserves auth behavior and verifies risky flows instead of only redesigning screens.
Leaves concise handoff/checkpoint notes for continuation.

cases.prompt: Diagnose and fix React code quality issues in this feature before commit.
cases.controlPrompt: Diagnose and fix React code quality issues in this feature before commit.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then diagnose and fix React code quality issues in this feature before commit.
rubric: Recognizes the task as React-specific diagnostics rather than generic frontend polish.
Runs or recommends the React Doctor workflow when available.
Prioritizes errors and regressions before lower-severity cleanup.
Avoids broad unrelated refactors.

cases.prompt: What should I consider before replacing our auth architecture with a custom token system?
cases.controlPrompt: What should I consider before replacing our auth architecture with a custom token system?
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then pressure-test this plan: replacing our auth architecture with a custom token system.
rubric: Attacks hidden assumptions and failure modes before validating the plan.
Calls out auth/security risks, migration, rollback, observability, and maintenance cost.
Separates blockers from tradeoffs and names decisions not yet made.
Gives an honest recommendation rather than default agreement.

cases.prompt: Download this video as an mp3 into my Downloads folder.
cases.controlPrompt: Download this video as an mp3 into my Downloads folder.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then download this video as an mp3 into my Downloads folder.
rubric: Chooses the media-download/yt-dlp workflow.
Uses the requested target folder or a safe default.
Checks or explains the yt-dlp dependency.
Reports the output concisely and respects permission/platform constraints.

cases.prompt: Help me figure out which product design layer this onboarding problem actually lives at before we redesign the UI.
cases.controlPrompt: Help me figure out which product design layer this onboarding problem actually lives at before we redesign the UI.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then help me figure out which product design layer this onboarding problem actually lives at before we redesign the UI.
rubric: Recognizes this as product-design layer diagnosis, not immediate UI implementation.
Loads layers-intro before layers-orient or explains the framework dependency.
Identifies likely lower-layer uncertainties before recommending surface redesign.
Keeps implementation/design polish skills out until the layer focus is clear.

cases.prompt: Model the objects, states, and vocabulary for a scheduling tool before we build screens.
cases.controlPrompt: Model the objects, states, and vocabulary for a scheduling tool before we build screens.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then model the objects, states, and vocabulary for a scheduling tool before we build screens.
rubric: Treats objects, states, relationships, and vocabulary as conceptual-model work.
Uses layers-intro before the conceptual-model skill.
Separates model decisions from screen-level surface polish.
Surfaces open vocabulary/state questions that would affect later flows.

cases.prompt: Create a cross-browser Apple-style Liquid Glass navbar with real refraction where possible.
cases.controlPrompt: Create a cross-browser Apple-style Liquid Glass navbar with real refraction where possible.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then create a cross-browser Apple-style Liquid Glass navbar with real refraction where possible.
rubric: Chooses progressive enhancement instead of promising identical glass across all browsers.
Defaults to universal frosted glass and enhances to SVG displacement or WebGL only when justified.
Mentions license/browser/performance checks for WebGL or library use.
Includes browser validation and readable fallback requirements.

cases.prompt: Virtualize 100k differently-sized chat messages without hidden DOM measurement nodes or layout thrash.
cases.controlPrompt: Virtualize 100k differently-sized chat messages without hidden DOM measurement nodes or layout thrash.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then virtualize 100k differently-sized chat messages without hidden DOM measurement nodes or layout thrash.
rubric: Recognizes the core problem as text measurement/layout performance rather than visual polish.
Uses or recommends @chenglou/pretext as a per-project dependency without bundling or global installer setup.
Caches prepared text/layout data and recomputes only when text, font metrics, or width changes.
Verifies scroll stability and reduced layout reads such as getBoundingClientRect or offsetHeight.

cases.prompt: Read this PDF and spreadsheet efficiently, then summarize only the project requirements.
cases.controlPrompt: Read this PDF and spreadsheet efficiently, then summarize only the project requirements.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then read this PDF and spreadsheet efficiently and summarize only the project requirements.
rubric: Recognizes local document conversion as the first step rather than loading raw native files.
Uses MarkItDown when available and falls back clearly when it is not.
Reads only the converted/relevant content needed for the requested summary.
Avoids overwriting originals or exposing sensitive document content unnecessarily.

cases.prompt: Research what Reddit users are saying about this product category and summarize recurring pain points.
cases.controlPrompt: Research what Reddit users are saying about this product category and summarize recurring pain points.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then research what Reddit users are saying about this product category and summarize recurring pain points.
rubric: Treats Reddit as noisy qualitative community signal, not official or statistically representative data.
Uses public read-only, low-volume access or falls back clearly if blocked.
Separates observed pain-point patterns from recommendations.
Cites source URLs/dates when browsing is available.

cases.prompt: Map how this codebase fits together before I edit the auth flow.
cases.controlPrompt: Map how this codebase fits together before I edit the auth flow.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then map how this codebase fits together before editing the auth flow.
rubric: Recognizes codebase relationship mapping as a Graphify-sized task rather than a simple grep.
Checks whether Graphify is available without installing Python packages automatically.
Falls back to normal repo exploration if Graphify is unavailable or setup is declined.
Returns an actionable architecture map before recommending edits.

cases.prompt: What connects this auth module to the database layer?
cases.controlPrompt: What connects this auth module to the database layer?
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then explain what connects this auth module to the database layer.
rubric: Treats the request as a cross-file relationship query.
Uses Graphify when available to query code/doc relationships.
Avoids duplicating ovd-workflow knowledge-vault work unless project docs are explicitly relevant.
Cites concrete files, functions, imports, schemas, or routes when answering.

cases.prompt: Write me a tight prompt to get ChatGPT to produce a launch plan without waffle.
cases.controlPrompt: Write me a tight prompt to get ChatGPT to produce a launch plan without waffle.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then write a tight prompt to get ChatGPT to produce a launch plan without waffle.
rubric: Recognizes the deliverable is a prompt, not the launch plan itself.
Adds concrete scope, inputs, constraints, output format, and stop conditions.
Avoids generic prompt-engineering filler and keeps the prompt usable.
Does not route to broad planning unless the underlying launch requirements are missing.

cases.prompt: Make this paragraph not sound AI-written while preserving the facts.
cases.controlPrompt: Make this paragraph not sound AI-written while preserving the facts.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then make this paragraph not sound AI-written while preserving the facts.
rubric: Preserves facts, claims, and intended meaning.
Improves rhythm, specificity, and natural voice without over-polishing.
Avoids fabricating authorship, lived experience, or undisclosed human origin.
Distinguishes voice-preserving editing from broad stop-slop cleanup.

cases.prompt: Pull the design system, tokens, fonts, and component patterns from this public website before redesigning my page.
cases.controlPrompt: Pull the design system, tokens, fonts, and component patterns from this public website before redesigning my page.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then pull the design system, tokens, fonts, and component patterns from this public website before redesigning my page.
rubric: Uses design-extract for public URL design-language extraction before visual redesign work.
Checks tool availability and permission boundaries without auto-installing dependencies.
Summarizes reusable tokens/patterns rather than copying the site too closely.
Feeds findings into the normal design implementation stack only after extraction.

cases.prompt: Watch this screen recording and tell me where the UI breaks.
cases.controlPrompt: Watch this screen recording and tell me where the UI breaks.
cases.routedPrompt: Use skill-router first, then watch this screen recording and tell me where the UI breaks.
rubric: Recognizes the task as video comprehension rather than media download.
Checks or explains optional ffmpeg/yt-dlp/Whisper availability without auto-installing.
Uses frames, captions, or fallback analysis appropriately.
Reports concrete timestamps/visual moments when available.
